s: 민주 (democracy) - a system of government where citizens hold power
q: What does the Korean word '민주' mean?
e: 민주 means democracy — a form of government where power is held by the people, either directly or through elected representatives.

s: 십상 means 'exactly right for / just the thing for' in Korean
e: 십상 means 'just the right thing for' or 'perfect for a given purpose.' Often appears as '~기에 십상이다' (it is just the thing for doing X).

s: 못되다 means 'mean / nasty / morally bad' in Korean
e: 못되다 means mean, nasty, or morally bad — describing a person with poor character or malicious behavior.

s: 대강 means 'roughly / in outline' in Korean
e: 대강 means roughly or in outline — a general overview rather than a detailed account.

s: 부제 means 'subtitle / secondary title' in Korean
e: 부제 means subtitle — a secondary title that appears beneath or alongside the main title to provide additional context.

s: 데리다 means 'to bring/take someone along' in Korean
e: 데리다 means to bring or take a person along with you — accompanying them or leading them to a place.

s: 자칫 means 'if one is not careful / one wrong move could' in Korean
e: 자칫 means 'if you're not careful' — it warns of a negative possibility that could easily occur with one wrong step.

s: 이삿짐 means 'moving goods / household items being relocated' in Korean
e: 이삿짐 refers specifically to the belongings packed and moved when relocating to a new home.

s: 맺다 means 'to form / to bear (fruit or relationships)' in Korean
e: 맺다 means to form or tie — used for forming relationships (인연을 맺다), bearing fruit (열매를 맺다), or concluding a deal.

s: 과일 means 'fruit' in Korean
e: 과일 means fruit — the edible product of a flowering plant that typically contains seeds.

s: 승부 means 'the contest result / who wins and loses' in Korean
e: 승부 means the outcome of a contest — who wins and who loses. Used in 승부를 가리다 (to determine the winner).

s: 몸살 means 'body aches from overexertion / general malaise' in Korean
e: 몸살 means general body aches and fatigue from overexertion or illness — the all-over achiness and weakness that comes with being run down.

s: 갈피 means 'a gap between layers / the thread of thought' in Korean
e: 갈피 means the space between layers or the thread of an argument. '갈피를 못 잡다' means to lose the thread of thought or be unable to get one's bearings.

s: 반열 means 'rank / standing in a hierarchy' in Korean
e: 반열 means rank or standing — one's position in a hierarchy or among peers. Used in '반열에 오르다' (to rise to the rank of).

d: a place well known to you

s: 한풀 means 'one notch / one degree (often of intensity easing)' in Korean
e: 한풀 means one notch or one degree — typically describing how intensity has eased slightly, as in 한풀 꺾이다 (to ease off one notch).

s: 육상 means 'land / track and field athletics' in Korean
e: 육상 means land or terrain. It also refers to track and field athletics (육상 경기), as these sports are performed on land.

s: 마사지 means 'massage' in Korean (loanword)
e: 마사지 is a loanword from English meaning massage — therapeutic rubbing and kneading of muscles to relieve tension.

s: 중계 means 'broadcast relay / live transmission' in Korean
e: 중계 means relay or live broadcast — transmitting a signal from the source location to audiences elsewhere, as in 스포츠 중계 (live sports broadcast).

s: 운행 means 'operation / service (of vehicles)' in Korean
e: 운행 means the operation or running of vehicles or transportation systems — 버스가 운행하다 (the bus is running its route).

s: 한창 means 'at the peak / in full swing' in Korean
e: "한창" means at the peak or in full swing — the height of activity, growth, or bloom.

s: 다름없다 means 'no different from / just the same as' in Korean
e: "다름없다" means no different from or just the same as — used to draw an equivalence between two things.

s: 마주 means 'face to face / directly opposite' in Korean
e: "마주" means face to face or directly opposite — two people or things directly facing each other.

s: 직후 means 'immediately after / right after' in Korean
e: "직후" means immediately after — the moment or brief period right after an event occurred.

s: 잠깐 means 'a brief moment / just a second' in Korean
e: "잠깐" means for just a moment or briefly. 잠깐만요 is a common phrase meaning 'just a moment, please.'

s: 여타 means 'the other / the rest (of the same kind)' in Korean
e: "여타" means the other ones or the remaining ones of the same kind — used in formal writing to refer to others of the same category.

s: 자체 means 'itself / in and of itself' in Korean
e: "자체" means itself or the thing itself — used as an intensifier or to indicate intrinsic nature, as in 그 자체 (in and of itself).

s: 폭등 means 'sharp rise / sudden surge in price' in Korean
e: "폭등" means a sharp or sudden rise — most commonly used for prices, stocks, or temperatures that surge dramatically.

s: 막상 means 'when it actually comes to it / in reality' in Korean
e: "막상" means 'when it actually comes to it' or 'in reality' — describing how things feel or turn out when the actual moment arrives, often contrasting with expectations.

s: 별도 means 'separately / in addition' in Korean
e: "별도" means separately or additionally — handled or considered apart from the main matter.

s: 아무래도 means 'no matter what / in any case' in Korean
e: "아무래도" means no matter what or in any case — expressing that the outcome is inevitable or that one suspects something regardless.

s: 큰소리 means 'a loud voice / boastful talk' in Korean
e: "큰소리" means a loud voice and also boastful talk. 큰소리치다 means to brag or talk big.

s: 재직 means 'being currently employed / holding a post' in Korean
e: "재직" means currently holding a position or being employed — actively serving in a job or role.

s: 천연 means 'natural / unartificial' in Korean
e: "천연" means natural or occurring in nature — free from artificial processing. Used in 천연 색소 (natural dye) and 천연기념물 (natural monument).

s: 빠지다 means 'to fall into / be immersed in' in Korean
e: "빠지다" means to fall into or become immersed in — used for falling into water, getting stuck in a situation, or becoming absorbed in something.

s: 들려주다 means 'to let someone hear / to tell' in Korean
e: "들려주다" means to let someone hear something or to tell them — sharing a story, song, or piece of information by speaking or playing it.

s: 낌새 means 'a sign / hint of something about to happen' in Korean
e: "낌새" means a subtle sign or hint of something — sensing that something is about to happen before it becomes obvious.

s: 한동안 means 'for a good while / for some time' in Korean
e: "한동안" means for a fairly long period of time — not forever, but long enough to be noticeable.

s: 지내다 means 'to spend time / to get along / to live through' in Korean
e: "지내다" means to spend time, to live through a period, or to get along with others. 잘 지내다 means to be doing well.

s: 어느덧 means 'before one knows it / time passing unnoticed' in Korean
e: "어느덧" means before one knows it — time has passed without being noticed. 어느덧 겨울이 왔다 = Before I knew it, winter had come.

s: 당초 means 'from the very beginning / originally' in Korean
e: "당초" means from the beginning or originally — how things were intended or planned at the outset.

s: 재학 means 'currently enrolled / attending school' in Korean
e: "재학" means currently enrolled and attending school — actively studying as a student at an institution.

s: 눈앞 means 'right before one's eyes / immediately ahead' in Korean
e: "눈앞" means right in front of one's eyes or immediately ahead — something that is directly visible or about to happen.

s: 의하다 means 'to be based on / due to' in Korean
e: "의하다" means to be based on, due to, or according to — used in constructions like 법에 의하다 (based on the law).

s: 국내 means 'domestic / within the country' in Korean
e: "국내" means domestic or within the country — referring to things occurring or available inside the nation.

s: 손수 means 'with one's own hands / personally' in Korean
e: "손수" means with one's own hands or personally — doing something directly oneself rather than delegating or using automation.

s: 임시 means 'temporary / provisional' in Korean
e: "임시" means temporary or provisional — something set up as a stopgap that is not intended to be permanent.

s: 불과하다 means 'to be nothing but / merely' in Korean
e: "불과하다" means to be merely or nothing more than — used to emphasize that something is less than might be expected.

s: 거머쥐다 means 'to grab hold of / to seize tightly' in Korean
e: "거머쥐다" means to grab or seize tightly — to hold onto something firmly, often something valuable like power or an opportunity.

s: 그저 means 'just / simply / merely' in Korean
e: "그저" means just, simply, or merely — expressing something done in a plain, unremarkable way or without any special reason.

s: 헤아리다 means 'to figure out / to consider carefully' in Korean
e: "헤아리다" means to consider or think something through carefully — to count, estimate, or understand by pondering.

s: 엇갈리다 means 'to miss each other / to cross paths without meeting' in Korean
e: "엇갈리다" means to cross paths without actually meeting — to just miss each other, or for opinions/paths to diverge.

s: 다닥다닥 means 'clustered tightly together / closely packed' in Korean
e: "다닥다닥" is an onomatopoeic adverb describing things clustered tightly or closely packed together, like houses crammed in a narrow alley.

s: 민간 means 'civilian / private (non-governmental)' in Korean
e: "민간" means civilian or private — the non-governmental sector or common people, as opposed to the military or state.

s: 표방 means 'to profess / to publicly stand for' in Korean
e: "표방" means to publicly espouse or profess something — to declare what one stands for or represents.

s: 여지 means 'room / margin / scope for something' in Korean
e: "여지" means room, margin, or scope for something — as in 의심할 여지가 없다 (no room for doubt) or 개선의 여지 (room for improvement).

s: 살기 means 'murderous intent / a threatening aura' in Korean
e: "살기" means murderous intent or a stormy, threatening atmosphere — the dangerous aura that someone exudes.

s: 사이사이 means 'here and there in the gaps / at intervals' in Korean
e: "사이사이" means at intervals or in the spaces between — something distributed throughout the gaps.

s: 물씬 means 'strongly wafting / reeking of' in Korean
e: "물씬" means a strong waft or thick smell — the sense of something strongly emanating from a source.

s: 공짜 means 'free of charge / at no cost' in Korean
e: "공짜" means free of charge or at no cost — getting something without paying for it.

s: 문밖 means 'outside the door / beyond the doorway' in Korean
e: "문밖" means outside the door — the area beyond the doorway or outside the room.

s: 고스란히 means 'just as it was / completely intact' in Korean
e: "고스란히" means just as it was or completely intact — passed on or returned without any alteration.

s: 잘되다 means 'to go well / to work out successfully' in Korean
e: "잘되다" means to go well or work out successfully — things proceeding in a favorable manner.

s: 상시 means 'at all times / on a regular basis' in Korean
e: "상시" means at all times or on a regular, permanent basis — 상시 운영 means operating at all times.

s: 하여금 means 'to make/let someone do (causative)' in Korean
e: "하여금" is a formal causative expression meaning to make or let someone do something — used in the pattern '~으로 하여금 ~하게 하다'.

s: 직전 means 'just before / on the verge of' in Korean
e: "직전" means just before or on the very verge of — the moment immediately preceding an event.

s: 지게 means 'A-frame carrier / traditional Korean back carrier' in Korean
e: "지게" is a traditional Korean A-frame carrier worn on the back, used to transport heavy loads of firewood, crops, or goods.

s: 구속 means 'arrest / restraint / constraint' in Korean
e: "구속" means arrest or restraint — being held or confined by authority. It also means constraint in general contexts.

s: 시동 means 'starting an engine / ignition' in Korean
e: "시동" means starting up an engine — 시동을 걸다 (to start the engine) is a common expression.

s: 수동 means 'manual / passive / non-automatic' in Korean
e: "수동" means manual or passive — operated by hand or not self-acting, the opposite of 자동 (automatic).
